item.title: Triad hires townie Bill Hickley as baseball coach amid community uproar
item.description: Board votes for lifelong local William “Bill” Hickley; parents petition, students cheer, and the parking lot at Steve’s Market has opinions.

item.title: Graham golf scramble raises funds and mild suspicions about scorecards
item.description: Charity event at the local course nets support for athletics and at least one alleged “foot wedge.”

item.title: Village council tables debate over fourth stop sign on Main Street
item.description: Mechanicsburg council members agreed only that something should be done, then scheduled another meeting to decide what.

item.title: Local man declares victory after mowing ditch before neighbor
item.description: A Mechanicsburg homeowner called the early cut “the Super Bowl of rural property management.”

item.title: ROAD WORK: Willow Dale detour now a lifestyle
item.description: County engineers say the project is on schedule. Drivers say their GPS is gaslighting them.

item.title: Sheriff reminds drivers that deer do not use crosswalks
item.description: Nighttime crashes climb as corn reaches “ambush height,” officials say.

item.title: PAWS CAT OF THE WEEK: Captain Crunch
item.description: Local shelter highlight — orange tabby seeks couch, treats, and limited vacuum exposure.

item.title: St. Paris fireworks still 'technically legal if you ask right,' say locals
item.description: Fourth of July weekend brought tradition, tinnitus, and one very tired dog.

item.title: CLUB NEWS: Rotary honors member who actually showed up
item.description: Local service club celebrates perfect attendance and excellent pancake-flip form.

item.title: Data center flyer war enters week six with no clear winner
item.description: Champaign County mailboxes fill with glossy promises and glossy counter-promises.

item.title: OPINION: Waffle House diplomacy could fix county politics
item.description: If you can share a booth at 2 a.m., you can share a zoning map.

item.title: The unofficial ranking of Champaign County porch sits
item.description: From Mechanicsburg swings to St. Paris stoops, we rate the art of doing nothing outside.

item.title: LETTER: Please stop parking like that near the school
item.description: A reader asks, politely but not that politely, for six more inches of common sense.

item.title: Fair preview: Funnel cake futures look bullish
item.description: Champaign County Fair returns with rides, livestock, and the annual debate over best fair food.

item.title: Harold J. Example, 1941–2026
item.description: Sample obituary format for the site — replace with real paid notices as needed.

item.title: SPECIAL SECTION: Mechanicsburg graduation, in photos and pride
item.description: Caps, gowns, and the collective exhale of a thousand relatives with cameras.
